mainEntity.name: How much does a Land Rover Discovery SD4 engine replacement cost in the UK?

acceptedAnswer.text: A reconditioned SD4 (204DTD) engine typically costs £2,400–£4,000 supply only. Fitting adds £650–£1,300 depending on workshop location. Main dealer new engines can exceed £8,000 supply only. Use our calculator for a figure specific to your car.

mainEntity.name: Is the Land Rover Discovery SD4 engine reliable?

acceptedAnswer.text: This is one of the more controversial engines in Land Rover's modern lineup. The 204DTD Ingenium diesel has a well-documented reputation for timing chain and turbocharger issues. Specialist rebuilders report seeing these engines regularly with catastrophic failures. However, with meticulous maintenance (oil changes every 8,000 miles) and preventative timing chain replacement, some examples exceed 150,000 miles.

mainEntity.name: What are the common problems with the Land Rover Discovery SD4?

acceptedAnswer.text: Timing chain stretch and failure (the most common and costly issue), turbocharger failure (which can cause hydro-lock and bent conrods), injector over-fuelling (which can melt pistons), and DPF clogging (particularly with urban driving). The engine is highly sensitive to oil change intervals.

mainEntity.name: Is it worth replacing a Discovery SD4 engine, or should I scrap the car?

acceptedAnswer.text: Apply the 60% Land Rover scrap threshold: if your Discovery 5 is worth £12,000 and a recon engine is £3,200 (27%), it's a clear keeper. If the vehicle is a tired £6,000 example, the same engine exceeds 50% — get a scrap valuation first. These are complex, valuable vehicles that often justify replacement spend.

mainEntity.name: How do I find my Land Rover Discovery SD4 engine code?

acceptedAnswer.text: The engine code is listed on the VIN plate and vehicle documentation. The SD4 uses the 204DTD (AJ20D4) — a 2.0-litre twin-turbo diesel producing 240 PS. Tell us your registration date and we'll confirm it for free.

mainEntity.name: Can I repair the 204DTD timing chain, or does it need replacement?

acceptedAnswer.text: Yes — if caught early. A preventative chain replacement (£1,500–£3,000) is far cheaper than a catastrophic chain snap requiring a full engine replacement. At the first sign of cold-start rattle, book an inspection immediately. Many specialists recommend replacing the chain as preventative maintenance at 80,000 miles.

replacementCosts.h2: Land Rover Discovery SD4 Engine Replacement Costs
replacementCosts.figuresNote: Figures above reflect the only engine generation this badge ever used — the 204DTD. See the Era Map above for era-specific pricing.
replacementCosts.labourEstimate: Fitting a Discovery SD4 engine typically takes 10–14 hours at £65–£95/hr (independent) or £110–£160/hr (London/SE dealer). The 204DTD is a complex modern diesel requiring specialist knowledge.

cta.label: → Compare Discovery SD4 engine prices from UK specialists

problems.title: Timing Chain Failure (204DTD)
problems.symptoms: Cold-start rattle, metallic ticking from engine, engine warning light, loss of power, rough running.
problems.typicalMileage: 60,000–120,000 miles — problems can appear much earlier .
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only if caught early; catastrophic failure requires full engine replacement .

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: at first sign of rattle, book inspection today.

problems.recommendation: The 204DTD's timing chain system is a known weak point. The components that make up the timing system are prone to failure because of the weak materials used . Plastic guides can break and drop debris into the oil pickup, causing spun bearings . If the chain stretches too far, it can "jump" a tooth or snap, causing pistons to strike open valves in this interference engine . Regular oil changes (every 8,000 miles, not the suggested 24,000) are critical .

problems.title: Turbocharger Failure
problems.symptoms: Loss of power, excessive smoke, whistling noise from engine bay, engine management light.
problems.replacementCost: N/A — repair only; turbo failure can cause catastrophic engine damage.

urgency.label: Immediate
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if symptoms appear.

problems.recommendation: The turbocharger on all diesel Ingenium engines is composed of components and materials that are seemingly not sufficient for its purpose . A failed turbocharger can cause engine failure due to the intake of oil and possible debris . When excessive amounts of oil pass through the intake, the engine hydro-locks, bending conrods . If you notice any whistling, smoke, or power loss, have the turbo inspected immediately.

problems.title: Fuel Injector Failure (Over-fuelling)
problems.symptoms: Rough idle, black smoke, poor fuel economy, engine warning light, loss of power.

urgency.label: Check
urgency.text: book inspection urgently if symptoms appear.

problems.recommendation: The fuel injectors fitted to the 2.0 Ingenium engines have a tendency to over-fuel . This produces an extreme amount of heat as the large amount of fuel is compressed and ignited . The piston itself is comprised of a weaker than expected material and is very susceptible to damage . Over-fuelling injectors can cause the piston to melt . Regular servicing and using quality fuel can help mitigate this risk.

repairBuyOrReplace.h2: Discovery SD4 — Repair, Buy, or Replace?

rows.whenItMakesSense: Preventative — catches fault before catastrophic failure
Replace turbo before it causes engine failure
Piston-valve contact means little is left to repair
Bent conrods and damaged pistons require full rebuild

buyingChecks.title: Buying This SD4? Check These First
buyIf: * Full service history with oil changes every 8,000 miles or less
* Timing chain replaced as preventative maintenance
* No history of engine warning lights or DPF issues
avoidIf: * Cold-start rattle or ticking — indicates timing chain issues
* Evidence of turbo failure (whistling, smoke, power loss)
* Poor service history — these engines are unforgiving of neglect
* Engine warning light with no diagnosis, regardless of mileage

repairBuyOrReplace.closingVerdict: The 204DTD is a complex, high-performance engine that demands meticulous maintenance. With its known timing chain and turbocharger vulnerabilities, buying one without a documented service history is a significant risk. A £3,500 reconditioned engine on a Discovery 5 valued at £12,000 is 29% of the car's value — worth it. On a sub-£6,000 example, think carefully. The 60% Land Rover scrap threshold applies.

cta.label: → Compare Discovery SD4 engine prices before you buy

buyingChecklist.intro: Before you buy a used Land Rover Discovery SD4 (or before you commit to an engine replacement), check these:
items: * Cold Start — Listen for rattle or ticking on startup (timing chain warning)
* Service History — Including oil changes every 8,000 miles (not the suggested 24,000)
* Turbo Check — Test acceleration; any whistling, smoke, or power loss?
* Smoke Check — Black smoke? (injector over-fuelling) / Blue smoke? (turbo oil leak)
* DPF Regeneration — Check history; short journeys accelerate DPF clogging
* Oil Level — Check for diesel contamination (oil dilution from DPF regen)
* Engine Warning Light — Have codes been read? Any unresolved issues?
* MOT History — Check for emissions-related failures
